description.en: A holding/input register address; Modbus addresses are 16-bit (0-65535). For 32-bit types, the LOWER of the two addresses (see word_order). Composed by scalar registers and categorical priority rules.
Addresses read by a flags decode; labels[i] is the bit map for addresses[i], parallel.
String published when no priority rule matches; omitted = no value / unavailable.
The string published when a priority rule matches. Named emit (not value) to keep `value` from naming five different things across atoms. Authored as the `emit` key.
String published when no flag bit is active; default "None".
Slug naming the fault-set slug group a categorical decode publishes into (fault_code). Exclusive with `state` (a run-state slug group) — an entry is one or the other.

Feature naming the feature-of-interest whose reading is meant (a property/feature/ code: pv_tracker, enclosure, grid, battery, ...). A value/gate/decode roots in a feature-of-interest, never a bare quantity (temperature of WHICH feature?). Validated against the archetype's projected feature tree.
Separator between active labels of a flags decode; default "; ".
The decode discriminant — flags or priority.

One label per bit, index = bit position (matched as value & (1 << index)); null marks a reserved/unused bit. 0-16 entries per register word; may be shorter than 16 (trailing unmapped bits omitted) or empty. Outer array parallel to addresses.
Bit mask ANDed with the register value in a priority rule.
Evaluated in order: first rule whose (register at address & mask) is non-zero wins; its emit string is published. Each rule = { address, mask, emit }.

A categorical-observable state group slug (run_state, operating_mode, fault_code), as opposed to a numeric quantity_kind. Declared by a categorical decode entry, referenced by a categorical condition gate — two sides of one channel.
